properties.remark: THREE MAJOR ROADWAYS IN AND OUT OF PALM SPRINGS WERE CLOSED DUE TO ZERO VISIBILITY CAUSED BY BLOWING DUST AND SAND. THESE ROADS WERE INDIAN AVE, GENE AUTRY TRAIL, AND VISTA CHINO.
BRIDGES AND WASH CROSSINGS WERE CLOSED AT WASHINGTON AND ADAMS STREET BECAUSE OF BLOWING DUST AND SAND REDUCING VISIBILITY.
REDUCED VISIBILITY FROM BLOWING SAND IS BELIEVED TO HAVE CAUSED A CRASH BETWEEN A BUS, A TRUCK, AND A SEDAN ON I-10 WESTBOUND NEAR THE GENE AUTRY TRAIL EXIT.
BLOWING DUST REDUCING VISIBILITY TO ONE TENTH OF A MILE OR LESS. WINDS 30 MPH GUSTING TO 47 MPH.
MUD COVERING HIGHWAY 78, E MILES EAST OF SCISSORS CROSSING.
WIND 30 MPH WITH GUSTS TO 50 MPH IN BLOWING DUST. VISIBILITY LESS THAN 200 YARDS.

LYTLE CREEK FLOWING AT BANK LEVEL. GREEN MTN. RANCH RESORT METAL BRIDGE WASHED AWAY. WIDESPREAD MUD AND DEGRIS FLOW. LYTLE CREEK HAS NOT LEFT ITS BANK LEVEL.CREEK IS 2 TO 3 FEED DEEP AND 25 FEET WIDE.
ROCKS AND MUD ACROSS ACROSS HIGHWAY 74 NEAR RIBBONWOOD DRIVE
ROCK SLIDE ACROSS SUNRISE HIGHWAY, S1, NEAR MILE MARKER 18.
HIGHWAY 18 BETWEEN LAKE GREGORY AND RUNNING SPRINGS, SEVERAL ROCK AND MUD SLIDES.

RELAYED REPORT FROM THE PUBLIC OF WIND DAMAGE TO A ROOF AT MCSWEENY ELEMENTARY SCHOOL IN HEMET. EXTENT OF DAMAGE IS UNKNOWN. TIME OF THE EVENT IS ESTIMATED BASED ON ANOTHER REPORT OF A THUNDERSTORM IN THE AREA.

WINDS FROM A THUNDERSTORM RIPPED OFF A 30 FOOT SECTION OF A MOBILE HOME CARPORT AWNING AND DAMAGED PART OF THE ROOF NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GILBERT AND CHAMBERS STREETS. A PIECE OF THE MOBILE HOME AWNING LANDED ROUGHLY 150 YARDS AWAY AT DIAMOND VALLEY MIDDLE SCHOOL. A TREE AT THE SCHOOL WAS ALSO DAMAGED. BASED ON SPOTTER REPORTS AND SURVEY OF THE AREA ALONG WITH RADAR DATA IT WAS DETERMINED TO BE A POSSIBLE GUSTNADO WITH GUSTS TO 65 MPH...ESTIMATED.

WINDS FROM A THUNDERSTORM RIPPED OFF A 30 FOOT SECTION OF A MOBILE HOME CARPORT AWNING AND DAMAGED PART OF THE ROOF NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF GILBERT AND CHAMBERS STREETS. A PIECE OF THE AWNING LANDED ROUGHLY 150 YARDS AWAY AT DIAMOND VALLEY MIDDLE SCHOOL. A TREE AT THE SCHOOL WAS ALSO DAMAGED.
M71MPH AT THE PIER ASSOCIATED WITH A WATERSPOUT.
SPOTTER REPORTED TREES KNOCKED DOWN, BARK PEELED OFF TREES AND POST OFFICE DROP BOXES FLIPPED NEAR WEIR CANYON AND THE 91 FREEWAY.
OFF COTTONS PT. 3/4 MI. S OF THE LIFEGUARD TOWER ON THE PIER. WATERSPOUT TOUCHED DOWN ON LAND BUT DISSIPATED.
A WATERSPOUT WAS SPOTTED OFF TEH COAST OF OCEANSIDE AROUND 4 PM. KUSI REPORTED THE EVENT AND SHOWED A PICTURE SENT IN BY A VIEWER WHO WITNESSED THE WATERSPOUT.
LIVE POWER LINES DOWN ON MARY LN. 1621L TREE DOWN ON ROADWAY NR MARY LN.
TREE LIMBS ON BEAR VLY PKWY AND RT78.
BIG ROCKS AS WELL AS 4 TO 6 INCHES OF WATER ACROSS THE STATE ROUTE 18 AND CACTUS ROAD.
LARGE ROCKS ON HIGHWAY 189 SOUTH BOUND 0.5MILES SOUTH OF LAKE GREGORY
LA POSTA ROAD FLOODED NEAR I-8. LAW ENFORCEMENT ON SCENE.
FLASH FLOOD AND MUD/ROCK SLIDE OVER COUNTY ROAD S2 IN SHELTER VALLEY.
ICSO ROAD IS FLOODED.
DILLON ROAD AND FARGO CANYON ROAD FLOODED.
WATER FLOWING ACROSS ROADS IN THOUSAND PALMS.

THUNDERSTORM WITH HEAVY RAIN AND HALF INCH HAIL STARTED AROUND 130 PM. ALTHOUGH HE MEASURED 0.68 INCHES IN ABOUT 2 HOURS...SWARTOUT CREEK IS NOW OVERFLOWING ITS BANKS. SOME CARS ARE TURNING AROUND...NOT WANTING TO CROSS.
SPOTTER HEARD ON HAM RADIO...CAL TRANS AND CHP HAVE CLOSED HIGHWAY 38 AT MILE MARKER 19 DUE TO A STREAM OF WATER RUSHING DOWN THE ROAD WITH EMBEDDED BOULDERS.

NORTHEAST TEMECULA. WIND ESTIMATES 60-65 MPH. A MICROBURST CAUSED DAMAGE IN A RESIDENTIAL NEIGHBORHOOD. THE MICROBURST DESCENDED FROM THE THUNDERSTORM DOWNWARD INTO THE MIDDLE OF A BACKYARD AND WAS ABOUT 20 YARDS WIDE BLOWING DOWN THE FENCES ON THE NORTH AND SOUTH ENDS OF THE YARD. THE FENCES WERE BLOWN OUTWARD FROM THE YARD INDICATIVE OF A DOWNWARD AND OUTWARD THRUST OF THE WINDS.
SEVERAL LARGE BRANCHES WERE BROKEN AND A FEW SMALL TREES WERE DOWN IN TRILOGY AT THE GLEN IVY RESORT AND SPA. ESTIMATED WINDS FROM THE DAMAGE WAS 60 MPH FROM A NWS EMPLOYEE.
TRW+A CAUSED FLASH FLOODING AND STREET FLOODING ON CENTRAL AND TUSSING RDS. SMALL HAIL LESS THAN PEA SIZE.
SDGE EAST WARNERS SENSOR RECORDED 58MPH GUST DURING TRW.
ON SR79 AND SAN FELIPE RD MM2 AND 3--MUD AND WATER FLOW THAT IS 3FT. DEEP.
MULTIPLE POWER POLES DOWN ACROSS THE RD. LANDED ON VEHICLES AND TRAPPED PASSENGERS IN VEHS. SEVERAL VEHICLES ARE ALSO STUCK BTWN THE LINES.

PUBLIC VIDEO OF LANDSPOUT TORNADO ASSOCIATED WITH AND SOUTHWEST OF PARENT THUNDERSTORM THAT WAS OVER NUEVO. TORNADO APPEARED TO BE OVER FIELDS JUST EAST OR NEAR I-215 AND NON SUPERCELL TORNADO. SEPARATE WIND DAMAGE WITH THE PARENT THUNDERSTORM WAS OBSERVED TO THE NORTHEAST OVER NUEVO ON MENIFEE ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED. NO APPARANT DAMGE FROM TORNADO. EF0 RATING.
LIGHTNING HIT A POLE AND CAME DOWN ACROSS THE RD. HAZARD IS ON RAMONA EXPWY AND BERNASONI RD.

WEST SIDE OF PERRIS ON OLIVE AND ELLIS STREETS AND JUST NORTH CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY 74...SEVERAL SMALL AND LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN. ON ELLIS STREET JUST NORTH OF CA ROUTE 74...TWO POWER POLES WERE BROKEN BY WIND BUT REMAINED LEANING. ONE RURAL STREET IN THAT AREA WAS CLOSED DUE TO PONDING OF WATER AT LEAST 12 INCH. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 50 TO 55 MPH FOR THIS AREA ONLY.

TIME ESTIMATED...WEST SIDE OF PERRIS ON OLIVE STREET AND CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY 74... SMALL AND LARGE TREE LIMBS DOWN. ON ELLIS STREET JUST NORTH OF CA ROUTE 74...TWO POWER POLES WERE BROKEN AND OTHERS LEANING DUE TO WIND DAMAGE. ONE RURAL STREET IN THAT AREA WAS CLOSED. ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 50 TO 55 MPH.

SBD194 HEARD FROM A POLICE SCANNER THAT A 3 FOOT WALL OF WATER CAME DOWN NEAR FOREST FALLS AND ANGELUS OAKS. POLICE EVACUATED PEOPLE THAT WERE DOWN AT THE BOTTOM OF THE SANTA ANA RIVER. ESTIMATED TO HAVE OCCURRED BETWEEN 230 PM AND 3 PM.
WINDS GUSTING TO 25 MPH BEFORE THE THUNDERSTORM GUST FRONT MOVED THROUGH. ESTIMATED 60 MPH WINDS...KNOCKING OVER PORT-A-POTTIES AT THE BUCK FIRE STAGING AREA.
THE CHP IS REPORTING A MUD...DIRT...ROCK SLIDE ALONG DEERHORN VALLEY ROAD.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS HEAVY RAIN CAUSING FLASH FLOODING NEAR OTAY LAKES ROAD. THEY ALSO STATED THAT THE BRIDGE NEAR THE FIRE STATION IS ABOUT TO BE WASHED OUT.

THE BUCK FIRE BASE CAMP AT DIAMOND LAKE EXPERIENCED STRONG WINDS...ESTIMATED TO 60 MPH...BETWEEN 140 AND 2 PM. THE WINDS DESTROYED 2 LARGE TENTS...A NUMBER OF CAMPING TENTS...AND SOME AWNINGS. THE PORT-A-POTTIES OVERTURNED AS WELL.
PILOT REPORT AT UNKNOWN ALTITUDE REPORTED GOLF BALL SIZE HAIL IN THE VICINITY OF HEMET.

FIRST REPORTS OF MUDSLIDES WHERE FROM VALLEY OF THE FALLS DRIVE AND LILAC ROAD...WITH MUD OVER THE ROAD. NUMEROUS SIDE STREETS ARE ALSO COVERED IN MUD...WITH UP TO 5 FEET IN MILL CREEK. AS OF 415 PM...NO RESCUES ARE BEING PERFORMED...BUT PEOPLE ARE STUCK IN CARS AND THEIR HOUSES BECAUSE OF MUD IN THE ROAD. NO INJURIES OR DEATHS TO REPORT.
HEAVY MUD SLIDE AND RAIN IN AREA.
MUD AND DEBRIS ON JAPATUL VLY AND LYONS RD INTERSECTION. TRW WAS STNRY OVR THE AREA. CALTRANS WAS CLEARING THE MUD ON THE RD WHEN SPOTTER SDG437 ARRIVED AT THE SCENE.
SBD129 REPORTS FLOODING IN MOBILE HOME PARK IN APPLE VALLEY. 2 INCHES OF STANDING WATER IN THE MOBILE HOME PARK. 0.62 INCHES OF RAIN MEASURED IN PAST HOUR.
SBD625 REPORTS DEBRIS FLOW THROUGH CANYONS NI HESPERIA ABOUT 1 FOOT DEEP AND 10 FOOT WIDE.
AT 215 PM PDT BORREGO COUNTY ROAD MAINTENENCE CREW REPORTED WATER AND DEBREE FLOWING ACROSS SAN FELIPE ROAD JUST NORTH OF HIGHWAY 78 BETWEEN MILE MARKER 12 AND 14.
CALIFORNIA HIGHWAY PATROL REPORTS ROADWAY FLOODING ALONG COUNTY HIGHWAY 22...OTHERWISE KNOWN AS BORREGO SALTON SEA WAY.

ESTIMATED 2 TO 3 FEET OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS HIGHWAY 79...A MILE WEST OF CHIHUAHUA VALLEY ROAD. IN ADDITION...TREES AND BRANCHES 3 TO 5 INCHES IN DIAMETER FLOATING ACROSS ROADWAY. PHOTO SHARED BY SPOTTER.
FLASH FLOOD AT CATHEDRAL CITY WITH RAPIDLY MOVING WATER THAT WAS 1 TO 2 FEET DEEP. SEVERAL ROADS CLOSED, INCLUDING EAST PALM CANYON AND ARABY DRIVE.
AN ATMO SCIENCE PROFESSOR AT UC RIVERSIDE ESTIMATED WIND GUSTS TO 60 MPH FROM A PASSING THUNDERSTORM GOING THROUGH CAMPUS.
ASOS MEASURED A PEAK WIND GUST OF 53 KT OR 61 MPH AT 247 PM PDT.
HEAVY RAIN RESULTED IN DEBRIS FLOW ON HIGHWAY 78 NEAR SHELTER VALLEY AND LIZARD CREEK. A 2 FOOT BOULDER HAD TO BE REMOVED FROM THE ROADWAY AND ONE LANE OF THE HIGHWAY WAS CLOSED. PICTURES TO COME.
SAN TIMOTEO CANYON ROAD CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING. SIX VEHICLES STUCK IN WATER. 1.53 INCHES OF RAIN FELL IN ONE HOUR AT MARCH AIR FORCE BASE IN RIVERSIDE.
SPOTTER RIV517 REPORTED STRONG WINDS OF UNKNOWN SPEED WHICH KNOCKED DOWN A PORTION OF A NEIGHBORS FENCE. WINDS WERE STRAIGHT LINE WITH NO ROTATION.
SWIFT WATER RESCUE ON NATIONAL TRAILS HWY AT LYNNHAVEN LANE. VAN WAS STRANDED IN 4 FEET OF MOVING WATER.
BARBOSA ROAD WASHED OUT.
LANDSPOUT OBSERVED AT 230 PM PDT NEAR PERRIS. REPORTED TO THE NWS BY RIV187. RECIEVED PHOTOS AND RECORDED VIDEOS.
2 TO 3 INCH THICK TREE LIMBS DOWN FROM THUNDERSTORMS IN LAKE ELSINORE AROUND 3 PM PDT.

OAK TREE BLOWN DOWN ACROSS LILAC ROAD NEAR INTERSECTION WITH SAN LUIS REY DRIVE. NEWSPAPER REPORT OF ADDITIONAL TREES BLOWN DOWN ACROSS LILAC ROAD NEAR INTERSECTION WITH COUSER CANYON ROAD - APPROX. 5 MILES NW OF VALLEY CENTER. TIME OF THIS SECOND OCCURENCE UNKNOWN.

NWS EMPLOYEE TOOK PHOTOS OF 3 LARGE PINE TREES DOWNED BY WIND. WEATHER SPOTTER REPORTED 40 MPH WIND AND PEA SIZE HAIL IN AREA. SDGE WEATHER STATION AT CUCA RANCH HAD 42 MPH GUSTS. THIS IS 2 MILE SOUTH OF PALOMAR MOUNTAIN AND ALONG HIGHWAY 76.
PALM TREE DOWN ON ROAD REPORTED BY CHP.
SPOTTER SDG956 MEASURED PEAK WIND GUSTS 53 MPH. VISIBILTY DROPPED AT 2008 PDT. POWER KNOCKED OUT. RAIN BEGAN AT 2114 PDT.
3300 BORREGO VALLEY ROAD. POWER POLE DOWN...WIRES AT CAR LEVEL.
STRONG WINDS AND LOW VIS FORCED SEVERAL VEHICLES TO PULL OVER ON HIGHWAY 86. WIND GUSTS AT THERMAL REACHED 49 MPH WITH A VIS OF 3/4 MILE AT 825 PM.

REPORTED FROM NWS SPOTTER RIV331. STRONG OUTFLOW WINDS...BLOWING DUST...HEAVY RAIN FROM THUNDERSTORMS STARTED OCCURING NEAR LA QUINTA AT 815 PM PDT. WIND GUSTS TO 61 MPH MEASURED AT 828 PM AND BLOWING DUST REDUCING VISIBILTY TO NEAR ZERO AT TIMES.
VISIBILITY LESS THAN 1/16 SM...300 FT
AT 830 PM PDT...BORREGO SPRINGS SDGE STATION MEASURED SUSTAINED 34 MPH GUSTING TO 60 MPH.
THERMAL ASOS REPORTED VISIBILITY BETWEEN 3/4 SM AND 3 SM AND WIND GUSTING TO 43 KT/49 MPH BETWEEN 2016-2044 PDT.
PALM SPRINGS ASOS REPORTED VISIBILITY 1/4 TO 1/2 MILE AND WINDS SOUTHEAST GUSTING TO 41 KT/47 MPH BETWEEN 2048-2153 PDT.
SPOTTER RIV230 REPORTED VISIBILITY 1/8 SM. WIND GUSTING 36 MPH.
LOTS OF DUST IN THE AIR. WIND GUSTS ESTD. 25 MPH

A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC AFFILIATED WITH UC RIVERSIDE REPORTED 3 INCHES OF RAIN IN THE PAST HOUR AT THE YUCAIPA RIDGE WEATHER STATION. MILL CREEK RUNNING 100 FEET WIDE AT ONE POINT WITH DEBRIS. POLICE ARE BEGINNING TO EVACUATE SOME PEOPLE IN THE FOREST FALLS AREA.
COMBINATION OF DEBRIS AND 6 INCHES OF WATER FLOWING ACROSS NATIONAL TRAILS HIGHWAY AT BARBOSA ROAD. VEHICLES WERE GETTING STUCK. CHP CLOSED THE HIGHWAY.
NATIONAL TRAILS HIGHWAY AT HODGE ROAD CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING.
HIGHWAY 76 EAST OF RINCON CLOSED FOR ONE HOUR FROM 330 PM TO 430 PM DUE TO FLASH FLOOD INDUCED DEBRIS FLOW 12-18 INCHES DEEP.
FLASH FLOODING DAMAGE SEEN AT THE CROSS-SECTION OF HIGHWAY 111 AND CLEVELAND STREET.
SPOTTER ORA130 REPORTED A WATER SPOUT A FEW MILES OFF THE SAN CLEMENTE COAST.
SAN CLEMENTE LIFEGUARDS REPORTED A WATER SPOUT OFF THE COAST AROUND 810 AM THIS MORNING. THE WATER SPOUT REACHED THE OCEAN SURFACE...PRODUCING SOME SPRAY.
DISAPATING WATER SPOUT ABOUT 200 YARDS OFF THE COAST OF THE CARLSBAD POWER PLANT.
NUMEROUS PILOT REPORTS OF WATER SPOUTS OFF THE COAST IN THE VICINITY OF NORTH ISLAND.
NUMEROUS PILOT REPORTS OF WATER SPOUTS OFF THE COAST IN THE VICINITY OF IMPERIAL BEACH.
WATERSPOUT REPORTED BY AIRCRAFT.

HUNTINGTON BEACH LIFEGUARDS CALLED TO SAY THAT TWO OF THE WATER SPOUTS WERE TOUCHING THE OCEAN SURFACE...PRODUCING SPRAY. IT APPEARS THAT ONE OF THESE WATER SPOUTS WAS HEADING TOWARDS THE HUNTINGTON CLIFFS...AND MAY MOVE INLAND.
LOS ALAMITOS SENT A SPECIAL AT 940 AM...REPORTING +FC IN THE OBSERVATION AND A WATERSPOUT BEGAN AT 40 AFTER IN THE REMARKS ABOUT 6 MILES SOUTH.

JOHN WAYNE AIRPORT ASOS REPORTED +FC IN THEIR OBSERVATIONS. REMARKS SAID TORNADO BEGAN AT 1754Z AND ENDED AT 1808Z 5 SOUTH OF THE AIRPORT. TALKED TO THE OBSERVER...HE SAID THAT IT WAS A WATER SPOUT OVER THE OCEAN AND NOT A TORNADO. IT REACHED THE OCEAN SURFACE FOR ROUGHLY TEN MINUTES PRODUCING SPRAY...BEFORE LIFTING INTO THE CLOUD AGAIN.
WATER SPOUT OVER THE OCEAN OFF OF SAN CLEMENTE PIER. THE WATER SPOUT IS CURRENTLY NOT REACHING THE OCEAN SURFACE.

THERMAL ASOS REPORTED VISIBILITY 3/4 MILE WITH WEST WIND GUSTING 40 MPH. MOUNTAIN TOP STATIONS TO THE WEST WERE REPORTING GUSTS OVER 60 MPH, AND AIRCARFT FLYING ACROSS THE COACHELLA VALLEY WERE REPORTING MODERATE TO SEVER TURBULENCE.

AN 8 TON BOULDER MEASURING APPROXIMATELY 5 FEET BY 6 FEET ROLLED DOWN A RAIN SOAKED HILLSIDE AND THROUGH THE GARAGE WALL OF A HOUSE IN THE 13000 BLOCK OF VIAN ROAD IN POWAY. THE BOULDER RUPTURED BOTH A WATER AND A PROPANE LINE. NO ONE WAS INJURED. PHOTOS FROM NBC7 SAN DIEGO.
SATURDAY MORNING 0945 AM 12/29/12 SAN ONOFRE REPORTS SEEING A FUNNEL CLOUD AND POSSIBLY A WATER SPOUT. DUE TO THE DISTANCE FROM THE OBSERVER,THE SPRAY AT THE SURFACE IT WAS DIFFICULT TO SEE.
955 AM A WATER SPOUT OR FUNNEL CLOUD WAS REPORTED WEST OF SAN ONOFRE POWER PLANT. IT WAS DIFFICULT FOR THE OBSERVER TO DETERMINE IF THE FUNNEL REACHED THE WATER SURFACE OR NOT DURING THE EVENT.
PILOT REPORTED A WATER SPOUT 19 MILES WNW OF OCEANSIDE.
SAN ONOFRE REPORT OF A WATER SPOUT OR FUNNEL CLOUD. EXACTLY DETERMINING IF THERE WAS SPRAY ON THE WATER SURFACE AT THE DISTANCE OFF SHORE WAS DIFFICULT
SATURDAY 12/29/12... LATE REPORT 935 AM WEST OF SAN ONOFRE THE OBSERVER COULD SEE A CLEAR FUNNEL CLOUD WHICH COULD BE TOUCHING DOWN... BUT DUE TO THE DISTANCE WAS UNSURE.

POSITION CORRECTED FOR VISUAL HORIZON OF 8 MILES FOR ELEVATION OF 39 FT/12 METERS MSL FROM SAN CLEMENTE PIER AND LIFEGUARD OBSERVATION OF BASE OF WATER SPOUT. PUBLIC REPORTED DURATION OF LARGE WATERSPOUT AT 15 MINUTES FROM 1120-1135 PST.
A LARGE WATERSPOUT WAS OBSERVED FOR 15 MINUTES 1120-1135 PST, ABOUT 10 MILES OFF THE COAST FROM SAN CLEMENTE.
WATERSPOUT FROM SURFACE TO 5000 FT MSL.
